After the Passover, the people of Israel worked hard to destroy the monuments of idolatry. Public services should motivate us to cleanse our hearts, homes, and businesses from the filth of sin, the idolatry of greed, and to encourage others to do the same. The lasting impact of solemn services is very important for personal, family, and public religion. When they had experienced the goodness of God’s service during the recent Passover, they were willing to support the temple service. Those who enjoy the benefit of a stable ministry will not begrudge its expense. In everything Hezekiah attempted in God's service, he was earnest and focused on his aim and dependence, and was prospered accordingly. Whether we have few or many talents entrusted to us, may we seek to improve them, and encourage others to do the same. What is undertaken with a sincere regard for the glory of God will succeed to our own honor and comfort in the end.
